relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Job answered the friends’ certainty.
He mocked their assumption that wisdom would die with them.
He too has a heart; he is not beneath them.
The righteous sufferer has become a joke to his companion.
Those at ease easily despise calamity.
Job points out that destroyers can live secure.
The animals can teach what the friends refuse to see.
Earth and sea-creatures also bear witness.
Creation knows the hand behind all this.
Life and breath of all flesh are held in that hand.
The ear tests words like the palate tastes food.
Age may carry wisdom.
But wisdom, strength, counsel, and understanding belong finally to the Divine.
What is torn down may not be rebuilt; what is shut may not open.
Waters can be withheld or released to overturn the earth.
Both deceived and deceiver exist within divine sovereignty.
Counselors and judges can be stripped of certainty.
Kings can be loosened and bound.
Priests and enduring powers can be overthrown.
Trusted speech and elder discernment can be removed.
Nobles and the strong can be humbled.
Deep things are uncovered from darkness.
Nations rise, spread, collapse, and are led away.
Leaders can lose heart and wander without a way.
They grope in darkness and stagger without light.
